title: abies concolor subsp. lowiana
intro: Abies concolor subsp. lowiana, also known as the Sierra fir, is a subspecies of the white fir tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and terpenes. The medicinal properties of this plant have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and even showing potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Abies concolor subsp. lowiana can be made into teas, essential oils, or infused into salves, and are often used to promote relaxation and ease colds, coughs, and sore throats.
